skill.name: vision-analysis
skill.description: Analyze images, screenshots, charts, documents, and UI mockups with the MiniMax vision MCP tool.
skill.summary: Provides MiniMax-powered image description, OCR, chart extraction, object identification, and UI review prompts.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, environment, and blocker patterns. Review found no executable source code or prompt injection, but confirmed publishable risk from third-party MCP setup, API key handling, external image processing, and optional local file access.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Setup Handles API Credentials
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to configure the MiniMax MCP package and pass MINIMAX_API_KEY through environment settings. This is a legitimate integration pattern, but users must trust the external MCP package and protect the key.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly documents MCP package execution and API key configuration. The context is setup guidance, so this is a real integration risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Image Content Sent to External MiniMax Service
medium_findings.description: The skill relies on the MiniMax vision MCP tool and MiniMax API host for image analysis. Images, screenshots, documents, and extracted text may contain sensitive information and can be processed by a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The MiniMax MCP dependency and API host are explicit. The skill purpose is image analysis, so privacy risk is likely, although no covert exfiltration is present.

low_findings.title: Local File Access Depends on MCP Permissions
low_findings.description: The skill notes that local file paths can work when MiniMax MCP has file access. This is expected for image analysis but should be limited to intended image files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file access behavior is documented directly. The evidence does not show unauthorized file reads by the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Static Command and Crypto Alerts Are Mostly Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Many static alerts are caused by Markdown backticks, setup examples, image mode names, and descriptive text. No executable Ruby, shell script, or weak cryptographic implementation was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown documentation with code fences and inline formatting. There is no executable source code that implements shell execution or cryptography.

dangerous_patterns.title: External MCP Package Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup examples instruct users to run or configure uvx with minimax-coding-plan-mcp. Users should verify the package source before installing or enabling it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and actionable. They are setup instructions rather than code that runs automatically inside the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: API Key Environment Variable Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup examples include MINIMAX_API_KEY placeholders. Users should store keys securely and avoid sharing configuration files that contain real secrets.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The API key variable is named directly in multiple setup examples. The values are placeholders, so no secret is exposed in the skill file.

content.value_statement: Image reviews can lose detail when handled manually. This skill routes image tasks to MiniMax vision prompts for description, OCR, chart reading, and UI critique.
actual_capabilities: Detects common image file extensions in user requests.
Guides use of the MiniMax_understand_image MCP tool.
Provides mode-specific prompts for description, OCR, UI review, chart extraction, and object detection.
Gives setup examples for OpenCode, Claude Code, and Cursor MCP configuration.
Defines output formats for image descriptions, extracted text, and UI design reviews.
limitations: Requires a MiniMax Token Plan subscription and configured MCP tool.
Cannot work with free or unsupported MiniMax API tiers.
Accuracy depends on image quality, readability, and model interpretation.
Local file access depends on the MCP server and client permissions.

use_cases.title: Review Product Mockups
use_cases.description: Analyze UI screenshots or wireframes for strengths, usability issues, and concrete improvement suggestions.

use_cases.title: Extract Screenshot Text
use_cases.description: Use OCR mode to capture visible text from screenshots, documents, or interface captures while preserving structure.

use_cases.title: Read Chart Images
use_cases.description: Extract chart titles, labels, series, visible values, and trend summaries from static chart images.

prompt_templates.title: Describe an Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a general image summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this image and describe the main subject, setting, visible text, important objects, and overall composition.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Visible Text
prompt_templates.scenario: OCR request for a screenshot or document image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all readable text from this image. Preserve headings, lists, tables, and line breaks where possible.

prompt_templates.title: Review a UI Mockup
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this interface image. List strengths, usability issues, visual hierarchy concerns, and specific design improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Data Chart
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for structured chart interpretation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this chart image. Identify the title, axes, series, readable values, data relationships, and the main trend.

output_examples.input: A dashboard screenshot with several panels and labels.
output_examples.output: The result describes the dashboard layout, reads visible labels, identifies key metrics, and notes any unclear or crowded areas.
The result returns the visible text in reading order and notes sections that are blurred, cropped, or unreadable.

output_examples.input: A line chart image showing monthly revenue.
output_examples.output: The result summarizes the chart title, axis labels, visible values, and whether the trend rises, falls, or changes direction.

best_practices: Remove sensitive information from images before sending them to the vision service.
Choose the analysis mode that matches the task before calling the MCP tool.
Verify extracted text and chart values against the original image when precision matters.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for private images unless third-party processing is approved.
Do not treat OCR results as legally or medically verified text.
Do not paste real API keys into shared prompts, tickets, or documentation.

faq.question: What does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes images, screenshots, diagrams, charts, UI mockups, wireframes, photos, and document images through MiniMax vision.

faq.question: Does it require MiniMax setup?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires a MiniMax Token Plan subscription and the MiniMax_understand_image MCP tool.

faq.question: Can it extract text from screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes. The OCR mode asks the vision tool to extract readable text and preserve visible structure.

faq.question: Can it review interface designs?
faq.answer: Yes. The UI review mode requests strengths, usability issues, and specific improvement suggestions.

faq.question: Can it read local image files?
faq.answer: Local files can work if the MiniMax MCP server is configured with the needed file permissions.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive images?
faq.answer: Use caution. Images may be processed by MiniMax services, so remove sensitive content unless approved.
